mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production signals an expansion in supply capacity that reduces the risk premium on equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate signals an improvement in labor market conditions that reduces the risk premium on equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in recovery periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts signals an expansion in demand for residential construction that reduces the risk premium on equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP signals an expansion in aggregate demand that reduces the risk premium on equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production and a 3-month decline in the unemployment rate signals a combination of expansion in supply capacity and improvement in labor market conditions that reduces the risk premium on equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% and UNRATE falls < 0.5% in 3 months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 3-month increase in real GDP signals a combination of easing monetary cycle and expansion in aggregate demand that reduces the risk premium on equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls < 0.5% and GDPC1 rises > 2% in 3 months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate and a 3-month increase in housing starts signals a combination of improvement in labor market conditions and expansion in demand for residential construction that reduces the risk premium on equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls < 0.5% and HOUST rises > 10% in 3 months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
